title: HORIZON.md is two-sided: a dream catalog AND a constraint set against which every shipped decision is checked
content: The catalog is reframed as serving two purposes simultaneously. First (the dream half), it makes the design space visible — what AMS unlocks, what becomes possible, what subscribers can be built. The catalog is wide on purpose because the vodka discipline of the wire produces a domain-agnostic substrate. Second (the constraint half), every entry is implicitly a forward-compatibility test: a v1 decision that forecloses any catalog entry is a wrong decision and must be revisited or the entry must be deliberately retired. SPEC.md §14 (Revision Discipline) is updated to encode this check formally — every proposed spec change is evaluated against HORIZON.md before commitment. The catalog protects v1 from painting future versions into a corner.

title: Comprehensive use-case enumeration enables decisive scoping rather than overwhelming it
content: The instinct on first encountering a wide catalog is to feel paralyzed by everything that "needs to be built." The actual function of the catalog is the opposite: by making the design space fully visible, the catalog enables the spec to be confident about what it is choosing not to commit to. We can ship a small first slice without anxiety about whether we're missing something, because the catalog is the audit of what was considered. The catalog is also the substrate for sales/strategy conversations: "we don't do all of this today, but we don't preclude any of it" is a stronger position than either "we do everything" (false) or "we do this narrow thing" (paint-into-corner risk).

title: Event-driven orchestration is added as §3.7 — the inversion of the dominant orchestrator-agent pattern
content: Orchestration becomes a property of the conversation, not a special agent role. Workers declare event triggers in stream metadata (via the well-known capabilities key); the wire's event flow IS the orchestrator; workers wake on event and react. This is the inversion of the LangChain/AutoGen/CrewAI shape where an orchestration agent decides who runs when, holds state about each worker's lifecycle, and is itself a single point of complexity, latency, and failure. The architectural parallel is cron-and-systemd vs event-driven serverless: the trigger drives execution, not a central coordinator. §3.3 (Hierarchical agent teams, the spawning-orchestrator pattern) is preserved as a contrasting valid pattern for tasks with known hierarchical structure; §3.7 covers tasks with reactive structure. Both compose freely on AMS.

title: The "missing link" framing names AMS's strategic position in the broader stack
content: AMS is described in HORIZON's opening as "the layer that unlocks the broader vision — the missing link between the agent infrastructure that exists today and the agent infrastructure that should." This framing positions AMS as the substrate that turns N point solutions into N subscriber patterns: every entry in the catalog requires a separate point solution without it, and becomes a subscriber pattern with it. The strategic implication is that AMS's value compounds across the catalog rather than being tied to any single use case — which is the structural argument for foundation-layer adoption.
